page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128 GB of system memory, providing a 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and is equipped with 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is delivered via a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and database performance. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 48,576.94 and a PassMark database operations score of 20,399.25. Redis SET operations reached 38.19 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 33.22 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with a Geekbench single-core score of 941.0 and weak single-threaded gzip compression and text processing results.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the im4gn.8xlarge offers high local storage density per resource unit, making it a viable option for storage-heavy workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute performance. The lack of AVX or AMX instruction support limits its utility for specialized vector mathematics. This instance is suited for distributed databases, transactional data stores, and log analytics applications requiring high-capacity local NVMe storage and moderate multi-core processing.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128 GB of memory, 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48,576.94 and 38.19 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 941.0 and lower single-threaded compression speeds. This instance is designed for storage-intensive workloads, including distributed databases, log indexing, and transactional data stores that benefit from high local storage capacity and moderate multi-core throughput rather than intensive single-threaded compute.

tagline: An ARM-based storage-optimized instance featuring substantial local NVMe SSD capacity and balanced multi-core performance for data-intensive workloads.

15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity data requirements
128 GB of system memory and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance with 48,576.94 stress-ng score
Suited for distributed databases, log analytics, and transactional stores
Tradeoff of lower single-core and single-threaded compression speeds
